select_when: A decision is being distorted by anecdotes, vivid stories, or tiny samples when population logic should dominate.
Repeated observations can reveal the signal beneath noise better than a single memorable case can.
Base rates, frequency, or convergence matter more than the intuitiveness of one representative example.
The team is mistaking a noisy short run for proof about the underlying process.
avoid_when: The sample is biased, structurally mismatched, or drawn from the wrong population, so large-N confidence would be fake reassurance.
Mean outcomes are being used as an excuse to ignore tail risk, distribution shape, or conjunctive failure structure.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model centered on signal-versus-noise discipline, population logic, and resistance to anecdotal certainty.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor-style statistics instruction beyond the raw source's stronger anti-small-sample and reference-class guidance.
Did not add higher-order fields because this batch stays inside the frozen Wave 1 to Wave 3 contracts.
open_questions: The source mixes statistical principle, risk framing, and expertise development, so the typed fields are reviewed operational compression.

select_when.note: The raw strengths section is explicit on anecdote correction, repeated observation, and base-rate or convergence conditions.

avoid_when.note: Both anti-activation conditions are directly supported by the source warnings on wrong populations and overemphasis on the mean.

input_type.note: The raw source activates when judgment is being driven by limited observations, but the compact input label is reviewed normalization.

output_type.note: The model corrects toward the population and distribution; the output label compresses that function into a typed calibration artifact.

reasoning_types.note: The source is clearly probabilistic and uses reference-class style analogy, but the reasoning taxonomy names are reviewed normalization.
